explanation: The original command is already a valid test command for the Sigma rule. No mutation is needed for the controlled test environment.
Replaced the double slash (//) as an alternative to the single slash (/) in the 'cmd /c' syntax. This is a safe substitution technique.
Inserted quotes around the executable name to test how the detection rule handles quoted paths, a common insertion technique.
Reordered the command-line arguments to test if the detection rule is sensitive to argument order, a safe reordering technique.
Used an escaped backslash in the '-t sch\task' argument to test path normalization and parsing behavior, a safe recoding technique.
